name: Isle of Glass
description: The Isle of Glass, often associated with Avalon, is a mystical and otherworldly island shrouded in mist and magic. It is a place of healing, knowledge, and transition, frequently depicted as the final resting place of legendary figures, most notably King Arthur. This enchanted island exists outside the boundaries of the mortal realm, accessible only through magical means or at specific times when the veil between worlds thins. Ruled by powerful sorceresses and healers, it is a sanctuary from the woes of the world, a place where wounds are mended, and wisdom is imparted. The Isle of Glass represents a connection to the ancient Celtic otherworld, embodying themes of immortality, rebirth, and the enduring power of magic in the face of a changing world. Its shimmering surface and ethereal atmosphere contribute to its aura of mystery and enchantment, solidifying its place as a cornerstone of Arthurian legend.
subtitle: A mystical island of healing.

keyMyths.title: Arthur's Final Journey
keyMyths.description: After being mortally wounded at the Battle of Camlann, Arthur is taken to the Isle of Glass to be healed by Morgan le Fay. Whether he will return from this mystical island remains a point of debate in the legends.
keyMyths.source: Le Morte d'Arthur, Sir Thomas Malory

keyMyths.title: The Apple Orchard
keyMyths.description: The Isle of Glass is known for its abundant apple orchards, symbolizing fertility, healing, and eternal life. These apples are said to possess magical properties, capable of restoring youth and vitality.

sources.author: Sir Thomas Malory
sources.description: A compilation of Arthurian legends, including descriptions of Avalon.
sources.text: A compilation of Arthurian legends, including descriptions of Avalon.

sources.author: Geoffrey of Monmouth
